channel.title: Pregnancy on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Pregnancy on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Preconception checkups being considered in Japan – recommends vitamin D above 30 ng (only 2% are above)
item.description:
Getting Vitamin D levels > 30 ng will:

  1. Reduce the need for fertility clinics

  2. Result in healthier mothers, infants, and children

Feasibility and effectiveness of preconception check-ups at workplaces in Japan

J Occup Health. 2024 Apr 29:uiae021. doi: 10.1093/joccuh/uiae021

Akiko Fujishima 1, Eri Maeda 2, Koki Sato 3, Hidekazu Saito 4, Chihiro Ozeki 5, Yukihiro Terada 1

Objectives: Despite the recent increase in infertility and perinatal complications, preconception care is not commonly available in Japan. Working women are considered to have the greatest need for preconception care, as they increasingly marry and have children later in life. This study aimed to assess the feasibility and effectiveness of preconception check-ups in the workplace.

item.title: Stillbirth rates dropped from 4.1% to 2.8% as Finland increased Vitamin D fortification
item.description:
VITAMIN D AND STILLBIRTH -OP No. 20

Anticancer Research May 2024, 44 (5) 2249-2268; DOI: https://doi.org/10.21873/anticanres.17034

Presented at Proceedings of the Joint International Symposia: Vitamin D in Prevention and Therapy and Biologic Effects of Light May 04-06, 2022 Schlossberg Hotel, Homburg/Saar, Germany

Pelle G. Lindqvist Karolinska Institute, Stockholm, Sweden

Background/Aim: Two retrospective studies of prospective cohorts showed doubled odds of birth asphyxia among women with low plasma vitamin D levels and one reported a 4-fold increase in the odds of stillbirth. It was not known if this was caused by low sun exposure or by vitamin D per se.

item.title: Morning Sickness might be eliminated by Vitamin D
item.description:
Hyperemesis Gravidarum is a severe form of Morning Sickness

Thalidomide was used as a treatment for morning sickness, among many other things.

Hyperemesis Gravidarum 40% more likely if low vitamin D - Dec 2023

Vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum: A mendelian randomization study

J Gynecol Obstet Hum Reprod. 2023 Dec;52(10):102678. doi: 10.1016/j.jogoh.2023.102678

Fang Hu 1

Background: The causality between vitamin D and hyperemesis gravidarum remains unknown. Our aim was to investigate the causal effect of vitamin D on hyperemesis gravidarum using the two-sample Mendelian randomization method.

item.title: Bone resorption during pregnancy reduced by weekly 28,000 IU of Vitamin D
item.description:
Prenatal but not continued postpartum vitamin D supplementation reduces maternal bone resorption as measured by C-terminal telopeptide of type 1 collagen without effects on other biomarkers of bone metabolism

Endocrine and Metabolic Science, Volume 14, 31 March 2024, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.endmts.2023.100154

Christine Krupa a, Huma Qamar a, Karen M. O’Callaghan a b, Akpevwe Onoyovwi a, Abdullah Al Mahmud c, Tahmeed Ahmed c, Alison D. Gernand d, Daniel E. Roth a e f

Highlights

  • Prenatal 28,000 IU vitamin D3/week reduced CTx at delivery, compared to placebo.
